name: CadStruct raster-only MoE drawing recognition model
purpose: Recognize non-vector architectural drawings from raster images and emit an auditable constrained scene graph.

hard_contract.inference_input: Raster image only. SVG/parser/expected_json geometry and semantic labels are forbidden as model input.
hard_contract.production_quality_target: Core precision/recall/F1 metrics should reach >=0.98 on locked evaluation.
hard_contract.architecture_principle: Domain-structured MoE: independent experts plus explicit relation/topology reconstruction and auditable constraint fusion, not one opaque monolithic VLM.
hard_contract.leakage_rule: Every field and feature must be marked as inference_available, offline_only, or derived_from_inference_available before it can be used by a production module.

developer_experience_policy.principle: Logical decoupling must not make the system harder to run, debug, or iterate. Architecture boundaries are audit and replacement boundaries, not a mandate to split the implementation into many isolated services.
developer_experience_policy.execution_model: Keep one-click aggregated execution for the common paths, with module-level scripts reserved for debugging, replacement, and focused training.

smoke_pipeline.purpose: Fast local sanity check across the whole raster-to-graph path.
smoke_pipeline.contract: Must run without manually chaining more than one command and must emit an artifact manifest.

locked_pipeline.contract: Must pin inputs/checkpoints/configs and write all eval/audit/review artifacts to predictable paths.

module_debug.purpose: Focused debugging of one stage without running the full pipeline.
module_debug.contract: Each module may expose a script, but it must accept the same artifacts produced by the aggregated pipeline.

current_aggregated_entrypoints.scope: image-only candidate -> crops -> experts -> fusion -> evaluation -> audits

implementation_rules: Prefer shared helpers and schemas over duplicate per-script field validation.
Every new module boundary must be accompanied by a default config and an aggregated pipeline hook.
A developer should be able to reproduce the latest smoke and locked reports from a single command each.
Artifact paths should be collected in a manifest so developers do not need to inspect multiple scripts to find outputs.
Audit traces should be generated automatically by shared emitters; developers should not hand-write provenance in every call site.
Do not introduce service boundaries, network hops, or package splits unless they remove real operational complexity.
non_goals: No requirement that every logical module becomes a separate package.
No manual multi-command workflow for normal training/evaluation loops.
developer_experience_policy.current_reset_decision: One-command v18 smoke/locked manifest runners are canonical control points. Long copied command sequences remain diagnostic until folded behind these runners.

image_only_v17_run_all.scope: image-only candidate -> crops -> existing experts -> fusion -> evaluation -> audits -> visual review
image_only_v17_run_all.developer_note: This is the current reference pattern for developer-friendly aggregation.

relation_graph_v18_smoke.developer_note: This is currently a command sequence. The next implementation improvement is a wrapper runner that executes it as one command and emits the manifest.

relation_graph_v18_locked.developer_note: This is the active P0 relation-graph path. It should become the next one-command runner.

full_raster_moe_v18_locked.command: TBD after candidate frontend, symbol type, OCR/text graph, and graph reconstruction gates are connected
full_raster_moe_v18_locked.scope: raster input -> candidate frontend -> expert inference -> symbol type -> graph reconstruction -> scene graph -> locked eval/review

cadstruct_moe_v18_smoke_runner.scope: canonical smoke control point for raster-only MoE; currently emits manifest and points to existing v17/image-only and v18 relation assets

cadstruct_moe_v18_locked_runner.scope: canonical locked control point for raster input -> experts -> topology -> scene graph -> audit

artifact_manifest_contract.purpose: One JSON document per aggregated run so developers can find every input, output, metric, audit, and review artifact without reading multiple scripts.

field_contract.inputs: List of objects with path, role, exists, sha256_or_size_when_available.
field_contract.checkpoints: List of model/checkpoint paths with model_id, exists, and checksum/mtime when available.
field_contract.eval_reports: Machine-readable metric reports used for gates.
field_contract.review_packs: Human review artifacts such as overlay JSON, HTML, or image folders.
field_contract.metrics_summary: Small stable summary only; full metrics stay in eval/audit reports.
field_contract.gate_decision: One of pass, fail, blocked, diagnostic_only, not_evaluated.
field_contract.failure_reasons: List of concise blocking reasons if gate_decision is not pass.
field_contract.source_integrity_summary: Raster-only and leakage status aggregated from reports.

artifact_manifest_contract.writer_policy: Every aggregated pipeline entrypoint must write this manifest even on failure, with missing artifacts marked exists=false instead of omitted.

SceneGraph.command: TBD scene graph validator over final fused graph

FeatureLeakage.command: TBD shared leakage scanner using struct.json feature_sources
blocking_conditions: offline_only field present in inference artifact

end_to_end_dataflow.design_intent: Recall-first raster proposal generation. This stage should over-generate plausible objects, but each proposal must preserve enough provenance for later canonicalization and graph reconstruction.
current_known_issues: space and symbol recall are still far below the 0.98 target on locked replay
patch heatmap symbol candidates improve recoverable gold but add too many rows per gained gold
room-wall supported repair improves room recall but remains non-adoptable
candidate failure types are mixed together: missing object, coarse object, shifted object, and cap displacement need separate audits

end_to_end_dataflow.design_intent: Normalize all detector outputs into one auditable stream before routing and graph construction.

end_to_end_dataflow.module: deterministic MoE router with learned-router ablation
end_to_end_dataflow.design_intent: Keep expert ownership explicit and auditable. Deterministic routing is the main model path; learned routing is diagnostic/future top-k support.
Give every expert local visual evidence plus lightweight page context while keeping provenance tied to the candidate id.
Run separately replaceable experts, each with its own dataset, checkpoint, calibration, and locked gate.

end_to_end_dataflow.module: symbol objectness, instance separation, and type classification
end_to_end_dataflow.design_intent: Do not ask one crop classifier to solve objectness, instance separation, and semantic typing at once.
Construct a high-recall relation graph before pruning. Relation selection should be learned as graph reconstruction, not only scalar confidence/NMS.

current_policy.no_regression_gate: preserve original relation recall, bridge positives, and at least one positive edge per gold_key

end_to_end_dataflow.required_next_architecture_change: Add wall-segment duplicate collapse inside each bounded_by room-side group so caps apply to segment groups rather than raw detector fragments.

end_to_end_dataflow.module: scene graph fusion and graph validator
end_to_end_dataflow.design_intent: Fuse expert predictions and selected relations into a legal scene graph with explicit invalid-rate checks.

end_to_end_dataflow.module: visual review pack and downstream export
end_to_end_dataflow.design_intent: Make every prediction traceable back to image evidence, candidate provenance, expert output, and graph-policy decision.

validation: route matches family mapping unless explicitly marked diagnostic
source names the expert checkpoint or rule
one candidate can emit multiple instances only with split audit
production-enabled type classes must pass locked precision/recall gates
source/target type constraints satisfied or warning emitted
no_regression gate result attached for locked eval

bounded_by.cardinality: many boundary segments per room; duplicate raw fragments must collapse to wall segments
graph_constraints: room should have enough side coverage
wall/opening fragments should not create unbounded duplicate support

contains_symbol.cardinality: a symbol instance should normally have one containing room; ambiguous cases require abstain/review
graph_constraints: preserve at least one edge for each recoverable gold_key

labeled_by_text.cardinality: many-to-few, with room labels and dimension labels separated by text intent
graph_constraints: room-label text should not be confused with dimension text

adjacent_to.direction: undirected node-node relation represented as directed edge pair or canonical ordered pair
adjacent_to.cardinality: sparse graph; bridge positives must be protected

final_scene_graph_relations.bounds: Canonical room/space to boundary relation derived from bounded_by.
final_scene_graph_relations.contains: Canonical containment relation for room/space to symbol/text-like child nodes.
final_scene_graph_relations.attached_to: Attachment relation for fixtures/openings to walls or rooms; may be produced by constraint fusion.
final_scene_graph_relations.labeled_by: Canonical semantic label link from text to target object.
final_scene_graph_relations.dimension_of: Canonical dimension text/line relation, separate from room label text.

wall_opening.current_metric_summary: README reports accuracy=0.993, macro F1=0.989, R2=0.980; upstream raster boundary recall is still below 0.98.
wall_opening.replacement_contract: May be replaced if it emits ExpertPrediction with the same label space and calibration report.

room_space.model_kind: context MLP plus text dictionary features
room_space.current_metric_summary: README reports macro F1=0.989 and recall@IoU0.5=1.0; candidate generation remains the limiting factor.
room_space.replacement_contract: Must keep room node ids stable enough for relation graph reconstruction.

symbol_fixture.model_kind: legacy 9-class tabular/tree classifier over symbol proposals
symbol_fixture.current_metric_summary: README reports macro F1=0.872; todo notes typed precision/F1 are far below the 0.98 production target in current objectness stream.
symbol_fixture.replacement_contract: Should be split into symbol_instance_detector plus symbol_type_classifier before production enablement.

text_dimension.model_kind: calibrated tree model plus OCR features
text_dimension.current_metric_summary: README reports standalone dimension macro F1=0.984 and relation F1=0.998; E2E remains dependent on localization and graph linking.
text_dimension.replacement_contract: OCR normalization, text localization, and labeled_by_text graph policy must remain separately auditable.

sheet_layout.current_metric_summary: Non-core extension; lacks real locked gold layout labels.
sheet_layout.replacement_contract: Cannot be promoted to core model until real layout gold exists.

relation_graph_policy_v18.current_metric_summary: Safe listwise policies preserve original relation recall but reduce only 0.4858% of relation candidates.
relation_graph_policy_v18.replacement_contract: Any replacement must emit SelectedRelationEdge with selection_trace and pass gold_key/bridge no-regression gates.

inference_available: page context derived from candidate stream
geometry features derived from candidate bbox and image size
component graph features derived from candidate/relation candidates
derived_from_inference_available: wall-segment group generated from candidate geometry
leakage_tests: candidate payload must not contain raw_label/base_raw_label/parser_raw_label/gold_candidate at inference
graph selection must not read gold_key or _label_positive at inference
review packs may include gold only when explicitly marked offline evaluation

candidate_frontend.fallback: emit high-recall stream with provenance, then mark budget overflow for graph policy

relation_candidate_construction.fallback: do not hard-drop recall-critical relations; mark overflow and defer to graph_no_regression_gate

scene_graph_fusion.audit_metric: invalid graph rate plus per-relation precision/recall

latency_resource.current_reference: README references reports/vlm/real_upstream_latency_resource_v1.json with replay/fusion P50=2654ms and P95=2691ms, excluding OCR/VLM/expert inference.

split_policy.dev: Allowed for policy selection and calibration.
split_policy.locked: Allowed for final evaluation only; no repeated threshold tuning after inspection.
split_policy.external_or_wild: No claim until human-gold labels exist for the target source.

candidate_frontend: no lost baseline recoverable gold keys
symbol_type: each production-enabled class precision/recall >= 0.98 or abstain-only

known_bad_proxy_metrics: gold-centered hard-case performance without inference-aligned generation
candidate cap sweeps that improve precision by losing recoverable gold

uncertainty_contract.principle: A module may abstain or route to review when calibrated confidence is insufficient; it must not silently emit production-enabled labels below gate.
review_triggers: multiple containing rooms with similar score
graph policy would drop a bridge positive under locked audit

candidate_frontend.question_answered: Did the raster detector produce enough usable object proposals?

expert_models.question_answered: Given a proposal, did the right expert classify it correctly?

graph_reconstruction.question_answered: Did graph reconstruction keep true relations while removing duplicate support edges?

constraint_fusion.question_answered: Did final graph assembly enforce legal topology without hiding upstream errors?

current_bottleneck_map.why: Candidate recall upper-bounds final node/edge recall. Current recoverable symbol recall remains far from 0.98.
current_bottleneck_map.next_design_direction: Dense seed/component supervision aligned with inference, plus detector reconstruction rather than patch-record AUC tuning.

current_bottleneck_map.why: Dense relation graph has high duplicate volume. Current safe listwise policies preserve recall but reduce only 0.4858% of relation candidates.
current_bottleneck_map.next_design_direction: Wall-segment duplicate collapse for bounded_by, then true graph reconstruction/link prediction with explicit bridge/gold_key constraints.

current_bottleneck_map.why: SymbolFixture standalone is below the desired 0.98 target and current symbol candidates are noisy/coarse.
current_bottleneck_map.next_design_direction: Separate true symbol type detector/classifier from objectness candidate generation, with hard-negative and long-tail audit splits.

current_bottleneck_map.why: Text expert is comparatively strong, but end-to-end text performance depends on localization and labeled_by_text graph links.
current_bottleneck_map.next_design_direction: Keep OCR normalization separate from text-box detection and graph linking so each error type is auditable.

p0_next_changes: Implement a shared pipeline runner that executes pipeline_entrypoints command sequences and writes artifact_manifest_contract manifests.
Add manifest emitters for image_only_v17_run_all, relation_graph_v18_smoke, and relation_graph_v18_locked.
Convert validator_registry planned entries into shared helpers rather than copy-pasted per-script checks.
Make relation_taxonomy the single source of truth for relation construction and fusion.
Add wall-segment duplicate collapse to graph_reconstruction.
Split symbol_fixture into symbol_instance_detector and symbol_type_classifier.
Add graph-level dataset and loss contract following graph reconstruction rather than post-hoc cap tuning.
Expose module debug commands only after they are wired into the aggregated pipeline and artifact manifest.
research_alignment: Raster-to-Graph style structural graph prediction for floorplans
GraphDoc-style document relation graph generation and graph-level metrics
self-constructing graph networks for learnable topology induction
panoptic symbol spotting with primitive plus image evidence
